substrates.substrate_A: indane 1 (functionalized indane with ketal protecting group)

mechanistic_details.key_intermediate: Br2 as photoactive species and brominating agent

chemistry.scope_summary: Single substrate transformation for manufacturing belzutifan intermediate

optimization_table.key_findings: 312 nm light, 1.5 h in DCE gave 89% LCAP

key_intermediates.name: compound 1
key_intermediates.structure: fluorinated aryl ether with dioxolane ring and methylsulfonyl group

key_observations_supporting_mechanism: Photo-bromination is highlighted as key transformation
Route proceeds from commodity chemicals to belzutifan through multiple functional group transformations
Sequential oxidation, reduction/deprotection, fluorination/DKR, and deoxyfluorination steps
figures.caption: Scheme 1. Overview of the Synthetic Route toward Belzutifan | Scheme 2. Process Comparison of the Thermal and Photochemical Approaches to Benzyl Bromide 2

figures.flow_path: single channel through coiled reactor with light irradiation
figures.key_information: Photochemical process using visible light (450 nm) as radical initiator with 2-4 min residence time at 25°C, achieving 89-95% yield
figures.caption: Scheme 1. Overview of the Synthetic Route toward Belzutifan | Scheme 2. Process Comparison of the Thermal and Photochemical Approaches to Benzyl Bromide 2

Figure 1.  In situ LED − NMR spectroscopy experiments. In both panels, the reaction was performed with starting material  1  (30 mM) and 1.05 equiv of DBDMH in CD 3 CN. For more experimental details, refer to the  Supporting Information .

key_observations_supporting_mechanism: Desired product forms via radical pathway from Br2 in presence of light and DBDMH
Undesired regioisomer forms via acid-catalyzed ionic pathway from Br2 in absence of DBDMH
figures.caption: Figure 1.  In situ LED − NMR spectroscopy experiments. In both panels, the reaction was performed with starting material  1  (30 mM) and 1.05 equiv of DBDMH in CD 3 CN. For more experimental details, refer to the  Supporting Information .

figures.trend: Product formation increases with time and shows optimal Cu content around 5-20 ppm
figures.caption: Figure 2.  (A) Relationship between the Cu content in starting material  1  and the reaction rate. (B) Impact of additives on the reaction rate. In (A) and (B), the reactions were performed on a 200 mg scale in an Penn Photoreactor M1 with 450 nm blue LEDs (1.05 equiv of DBDMH, 5 mol % additive where applicable, total input 3.40 W, 20% intensity, stirring at 350 rpm, and a 5200 rpm cooling fan rat

figures.trend: Conversion increases with time and photon equivalents, plateauing around 85-90%
figures.caption: Figure 3.  (A, B) Batch experiments: product LCAP as a function of (A) time or (B) number of photon equivalents. The reactions were performed on a 50 g scale with 1.05 equiv of DBDMH and 5 mol % citric acid monohydrate in 22 volumes of MeCN (1.1 L) in a 2 L glass jacketed reactor. The jacket temperature was set to 20  ° C, and the reactor was irradiated with four LED chips (64 or 120 W output powe

Figure 4.  Overview of scale-up in a tubular PFR. (A) Photograph of the reactor immersed in a  fish tank and irradiated with two LED panels. For setup details and procedure, see the  Supporting Information . (B) Product conversion over time and a table showing an overview of the results.

Figure 5.  Overview of scale-up in a tubular PFR in the pilot plant. (A) From left to right: close-up of the tubular reactor in its housing and surrounded by cooling liquid, the LED skid, and the tubular reactor positioned within the LED skid. (B) Steady-state reaction parameters over time, including temperatures at di fferent positions, the LED power, and the mass  flow rate over an extended run.

translation_logic.batch_limitation: Long reaction time (180 min) and low concentration (0.03 M) in batch mode
translation_logic.flow_advantage: Dramatic reduction in residence time to 3.75 min while achieving higher yield (94% vs 89%) and throughput
translation_logic.what_changed: Residence time reduced from 180 to 3.75 min, yield increased from 89% to 94%, scale increased from 1.05 to 3.25 mmol
translation_logic.what_stayed_same: LED light source at 450 nm, similar temperature (25°C to 20°C), same reagent stoichiometry
translation_logic.safety_improvement: Continuous flow operation eliminates need for azo radical initiators and provides better temperature control

figure_descriptions: Reactor scheme showing coiled tubular reactor with external LED illumination
Mechanistic schemes for synthetic route and photobromination mechanism
Optimization plots showing effect of Cu content and photon equivalents on yield

conflicts: Figure caption mentions 2-4 min residence time while text states 3.75 min - using text value as more precise
notes: Well-documented batch-to-flow translation with clear quantitative improvements. Missing some flow parameters like exact flow rates and back pressure, but key performance metrics are clearly reported. Time reduction factor calculated as 180/3.75 = 48. Yield improvement calculated as (94-89)/89 * 100 = 5.6%.
